FORM 10-K

PART I

Some introductory text about the company and its operations.

Item 1. Business

The company is engaged in the manufacture and sale of consumer products across North America and Europe.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

The following risk factors could materially affect our business, financial condition or results of operations.

The Company faces significant competition in all of its markets from both established companies and new market entrants. Competitive pressures may require the Company to reduce prices or increase spending on marketing and product development.

The Company is subject to extensive environmental regulations governing air emissions, water discharges and the handling of hazardous materials. The Company has received notices of violation from the Environmental Protection Agency related to emissions at two of its manufacturing facilities, and remediation costs are estimated at $15 million to $25 million.

The Company's workforce includes a significant number of unionized employees. Recent labor negotiations have been contentious, and the Company faces the risk of work stoppages or strikes that could disrupt operations and result in increased labor costs.

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments

None.

Item 2. Properties

The Company owns and leases various properties used in its operations.
